vuurtoreneiland.e: Compound of vuurtoren (“lighthouse”) and eiland (“island”). The island hosts a lighthouse.

vermout.e: From French vermout, from German Wermut. Doublet of weermoed.

examples: Systematische fouten vertekenen de resultaten.
Fans die via de website een kaartje probeerden te bemachtigen kwamen ook van een koude kermis thuis.

vervaeke.e: Contraction of van der Vaeke; see ver-. Appellative of Middle Dutch vake (“wattle dam in a river”), and see Vake, part of Maldegem, a municipality in East Flanders.

vroonland.e: From Middle Dutch vrôonlant. Equivalent to vroon + land.

voorne aan zee.e: Derived from the toponym Voorne, aan (“on”) and zee (“sea”).

van goeden huize.e: Literally, “from [a] good house”, using now-obsolete dative nominal forms, where "house" is used metonymically to refer to one's family and upbringing.

van slechten huize.e: Literally, “from [a] bad house”, using now-obsolete dative nominal forms, where "house" is used metonymically to refer to one's family and upbringing.

vrijthof.e: Inherited from Middle Dutch vrithof. Compare German Friedhof.

verzekeringnemer.e: Compound of verzekering + nemer. Compare German Versicherungsnehmer.

vleug.e: From Middle Dutch vloge (“flight”) (the genitive of which yields modern fluks), from Old Dutch *flugi, from Proto-West Germanic [Term?], from Proto-Germanic *flugiz.

volleybal.e: Borrowed from English volleyball. Equivalent to volley + bal.

volksrepubliek donetsk.e: Calque of Russian Доне́цкая Наро́дная Респу́блика (Donéckaja Naródnaja Respúblika) and Ukrainian Донецька народна республіка (Donecʹka narodna respublika).

van helsdingen.e: From van (“from”) + Helsdingen (“a hamlet in Vijfheerenlanden, Utrecht, Netherlands.”).

Als ik dit nu neerpen, denk ik terug aan een moment op de middelbare school. van haver tot gort kennen.e: Alteration of earlier van haver tot haver kennen, which in turn represents a folk-etymological alteration of Middle Dutch van aver te avere, meaning "from descendant to descendant" (i.e. "from father to son"; see Proto-Germanic *aferô), a form which seems to have survived dialectally into Modern ...

s.def: To say with a sigh (of emotion, such as relief or disappointment).

verzuchten.e: From Middle Dutch versuchten. By surface analysis, ver- + zuchten.

verstrooien.e: From Middle Dutch verstrooyen. By surface analysis, ver- + strooien.

vermaledijen.e: From Middle Dutch vermālediën. By surface analysis, ver- + maledijen.

examples: Ik ben van plan om naar het platteland te verhuizen.
Ze waren nog lang niet van plan om te gaan slapen.

van plan zijn.e: Literally, “to be of [a] plan”. Compare English of a mind.

examples: We trakteren, vooropgesteld dat we voor het examen slagen.

s.def: ab intra, from inside, from within
examples: Die nectarines waren al van binnenuit rot voordat ze rijp waren.

s.def: a measure, section, or piece in 4/4 time
to extract peat from a peat bog
a place where peat is or has been extracted
One who extracts peat for a living.

veender.e: From venen (“to extract peat”) + -er, with regular epenthetic -d- (compare tuinder, diender, and so forth).

s.def: a place where peat is extracted
the act or business of extracting peat

veenderij.e: From veender (“one who extracts peat”) + -ij or alternatively from veen (“peat, peat bog”) or venen (“to extract peat”) + -erij.

s.def: A polder reclaimed from a manmade body of water that formed due to the extraction of peat from a peat bog.

verveningspolder.e: From vervening (“a place where peat is or has been extracted”) + -s- + polder (“polder, area reclaimed from water”). The process of peat extraction often leads to the formation of pools and lakes, which in turn have historically been targets for land reclamation through the creation of polders.

s.def: frost line (depth to which water in soil freezes in winter)
to Hollandize (make or become more Hollandic)
A former man-made island in the Amstel river and former neighborhood in Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands
of or pertaining to Veere (a town in Zeeland, The Netherlands)

veerse.e: See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.

s.def: Dutchification; the act or process of making something more Dutch

examples: De zanger was ook op dit festival van de partij.

s.def: To become more Dutch; to assimilate or integrate into Dutch culture and society.

verkazen.e: From ver- -en + kaas (“cheese”); the derogatory sense derives from the association of cheese with ethnic Dutch people (see kaaskop).

s.def: To turn into soil; become soil-like.
To be buried in such a way that the body decomposes in a natural way, to serve as compost in nature.

veraarden.e: From ver- + aarden, from aard (“nature, disposition”).

s.def: To give shape or form; to design.

vormgeven.e: Compound of vorm (“shape, form”) + geven (“to give”).

s.def: alternative form of van de oude stempel
interpreter, conveyer; agent noun of vertolken (“to interpret, render, convey”)

examples: Zijn naam is om veiligheidsredenen weggelaten.

veelmannerij.e: From veel (“many”) + man (“man, husband”) + -erij (“ery”, suffix indicating an activity).

verfriezen.e: From ver- -en + Fries. Compare West Frisian ferfryskje.

s.def: The act or process of Frisianizing; Frisianization.
in my name, on my behalf

van mijnentwege.e: From Middle Dutch van minen wege. Compare German meinetwegen.

vogelmuur.e: From vogel (“bird”) + muur (“any of various small plants of the Caryophyllaceae family”).

vriendeloos.e: From Middle Dutch vriendeloos, from Old Dutch [Term?], from Proto-West Germanic [Term?]; compare Old High German friuntlōs, Old English frēondlēas. By surface analysis, vriend + -e- + -loos.

examples: Hij gebruikte een boor om het gat wat te verruimen.

verlating.e: From Middle Dutch verlatinge. By surface analysis, verlaten (“to abandon, leave”) + -ing.

s.def: something (process, plan, etc.) consisting of four distinct stages
to hold in front (of someone or something); to present

examples: Hij hield zichzelf voor dat hij een geboren kunstenaar was.

vervolgstap.e: Compound of vervolg (“follow-up, continuation, sequel”) + stap (“step”).

voorhanden.e: From Middle Dutch voorhanden. By surface analysis, univerbation of voor + handen.

s.def: many years old, of many years

verkonden.e: From Middle Dutch verconden, from Old Dutch farcunden. By surface analysis, ver- + konden.

vorstenlanden.e: See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.

s.def: to preprogram (predispose to certain thoughts or behaviours)
from a very long time ago; prehistoric; before the foundation of the world.
peace sign (hand gesture of peace, made with the middle finger and index finger)
peace sign (symbol consisting of three lines within a circle, originally the logo of the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament)

examples: Het poststempel vermeldt aan de linkerzijde „World City” en in het midden zijn als vredestekenen twee palmtakken afgebeeld.

s.def: peace sign (hand gesture of peace, made with the middle finger and index finger)

verluchten.e: Learned borrowing from Middle Dutch verluchten (“to illuminate”), a by-form of verlichten. This Middle Dutch form was revived in the 19th century in its current, narrower sense. Doublet of verlichten, and unrelated to lucht (“air”).

s.def: maidenhair, Venus hair fern (Adiantum capillus-veneris and similar ferns)

venushaar.e: Compound of Venus (“Venus”) + haar (“hair”), referring to the attractive and delicate hair-like leaves. Compare the taxonomic name capillus-veneris and English Venus hair fern.

s.def: princely state; (Indonesia) one of four nominally autonomous territories in Central Java administered by native rulers

vorstenland.e: Compound of vorst (“prince, sovereign”) + -en- + land (“terrritory, realm”).

s.def: a usually important event (or events) that the speaker considers irrelevant because they aren't affected by it personally, often because they happen far away from home.
examples: De crisis in Cambodja is mij maar een ver-van-mijn-bedshow.

ver-van-mijn-bedshow.e: Literally, “far away from my bed show”. Named so after a television programme with the same name (without the hyphens), effectively a combination of ver van mijn bed (“not relevant to me”) + show (“show”).

s.def: lead singer, main vocalist, especially in Afro-Surinamese music genres that employ call and response singing, such as kawina and kaseko
examples: “We heben weer geschiedenis geschreven om als eerste kasekoformatie uit Suriname op te treden op dit toonaangevende Nederlandse festival”, reageert voorzanger tevens bandleider Michael Deira trots in gesprek met de Ware Tijd.

voorzanger.e: From voorzingen + -er, with ablaut as in zanger.

s.def: synonym of van alles en nog wat

examples: De waarschuwing is voor dovemansoren geweest.
Spreek ik voor dovemansoren of hebben jullie me begrepen?
Coordinate term: bloggen

vloggen.e: Borrowed from English vlog. By surface analysis, vlog + -en.

s.def: an act or instance of corruption or decadence
someone knowledgeable on a given subject, who is willing and able to answer a lot of questions
to TikTokify; to adapt to TikTok
rest home, nursing home (small-scale, home-like place of residence that provides assistance with daily activities and GP-type medical care to people who require low-dependency care)
Any fern of the family Salviniaceae, particularly the watermosses (Salvinia spp.).

vlotvaren.e: Compound of vlot (“raft”) + varen (“fern”); these ferns float in water.

examples: Jarenlange corruptie in de lokale politiek mondde uit in een schandaal van jewelste.

van jewelste.e: The element jewelste is not encountered outside this phrase and represents a univerbation of je (“your”) + welste (“best”) (the non-univerbated equivalent van je welste is less common). The whole phrase can thus be translated, roughly, as "of your best".

s.def: to take some doing, to require a lot of effort
examples: De oud-president heeft zich er uiteindelijk bij neergelegt dat hij uit het ambt werd gezet, maar het had veel voeten in de aarde.

veel voeten in de aarde hebben.e: Literally, “to have many feet in the earth”. Origin unclear.
f: heel wat voeten in de aarde hebben (alternative)
de nodige voeten in de aarde hebben (alternative)

examples: Hij heeft zijn tegenstander uitvoerig van repliek gediend.

voor jan met de korte achternaam.e: Literally, “for John with the short surname”, a variation voor Jan Lul (literally “for John Cock”), Lul being the "short surname" which is euphemistically referred to by this phrase.

examples: Laat je niet van de wijs brengen door die idioot.

s.def: In a difficult situation and far from reaching one's goal; in a pickle.
examples: Zonder de steun van de anderen zijn we ver van huis.
Als je de boete niet betaalt ben je straks nog verder van huis.

superlative degree of ver van huis

vals judasoor.e: Literally, “false wood ear”, to be contrasted with echt judasoor.

s.def: to teetotal, to abstain from alcohol

van de blauwe knoop zijn.e: Literally, “to be of the blue button”, a blue botton being a symbol of teetotalism.

valse roos van jericho.e: Literally, “false rose of Jericho”, a reference to the true rose of Jericho (Anastatica hierochuntica, roos van Jericho), a tumbleweed which similarly appears to come to life after exposure to moisture.

s.def: first-person singular dependent-clause present indicative of voorstemmen

valsheid.e: From Middle Dutch valschheit. By surface analysis, vals + -heid.

s.def: vaccine skeptic, vaccine hesitant person (one who doubts or opposes vaccines)
examples: […] politici zien electorale kansen door zich te presenteren als vaccintwijfelaar.

s.def: acronym of Vereniging voor Vreemdelingenverkeer (“Association for Tourism”), a network of tourist offices in the Netherlands
to put someone in another, freshly made bed

veelbesproken.e: Univerbation of veel + besproken (past participle of bespreken).

s.def: act of judging people by their appearance

voorvel.e: From voor (“fore”) + vel (“skin”). Compare Gothic 𐍆𐌰𐌿𐍂𐌰𐍆𐌹𐌻𐌻𐌹 (faurafilli).

s.def: twenty-five-year-old, person of the age of twenty-five
a portable water container commonly used by soldiers; a canteen
indicates the speaker's utter contempt for the preceding thing, person, concept, etc.
examples: Wat een woordenboek van mijn voeten.

s.def: alternative form of van der Lee
perfecting (bringing of something to a state of perfection)
perfecting (bringing of something to a state of perfection)
loser (person who loses; one who fails to win)

vanwaaruit.e: Univerbation of van (“from”) + waaruit (“out of which”).

examples: Er is hier dichtbij een uitkijkpunt vanwaaraf je de hele stad kan zien.
